page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores utilizing the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture with AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type, meaning storage requirements must be handled via network-attached EBS volumes.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, achieving top-tier ratings in both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. The instance also demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, sits in the strong to average ranges.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 Graviton architecture delivers high resource density and exceptional multi-core throughput per resource unit, making it highly efficient for scaled parallel workloads. The massive 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth makes it ideal for network-heavy applications. Recommended workloads include high-throughput database hosting, distributed caching systems like Redis, static web serving at scale, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and 96 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. Equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, it maintains a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It features an impressive 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database and Redis SET operations. Qualitatively, the ARM64 architecture offers excellent multi-core resource density and efficiency. This server is highly suited for high-throughput database operations, large-scale static web serving, software compilation, and network-intensive distributed workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 384 GB of RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.24xlarge is a 96-core ARM64 general-purpose instance with 384 GB RAM and 300 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth ARM64 platform delivering 96 dedicated physical cores and 300 Gbps networking for demanding multi-core and database workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for high-throughput databases, Redis caching, and web serving
